name: Step Up Acadiana
description_short: Non-profit HUD-approved housing counselor in Lafayette, Louisiana providing free homeownership education and pre-purchase counseling.
description_long:
Step Up Acadiana is a non-profit organization based in Lafayette, Louisiana dedicated to helping prospective homebuyers achieve financial readiness for home ownership. Founded to address barriers to homeownership, the organization serves individuals and families in the Acadiana region who want to understand the financial and practical requirements of buying a home.

The organization provides several core services focused on pre-purchase readiness: free pre-purchase counseling via one-on-one sessions, pre-foreclosure counseling, high-cost loan counseling, and homeownership education. During counseling sessions, clients work with HUD-approved housing counselors to review income and expenses, create budgets, and develop savings plans. The organization also offers soft credit pulls for a small fee to help clients understand their credit scores and identify improvement opportunities before applying for mortgages.

Step Up Acadiana distinguishes itself through its staff of HUD-approved housing counselors with direct industry experience. The organization's stated goal is to bring clients to "100% readiness to receive pre-qualification from a loan officer," positioning counseling as preparation for actual lending rather than lending itself. Their intake process is straightforward—applicants fill out a form and receive a call to schedule appointments. The organization emphasizes personalized coaching from experts rather than generic financial education.

A critical assessment: Step Up Acadiana is genuinely a free-help organization that matches its category precisely. However, the website provides limited detail about service scope, eligibility requirements, geographic service boundaries, or outcomes data. The organization does not appear to offer down payment assistance or grants directly—only counseling about accessing external programs. Potential clients should contact directly to confirm they serve their specific location and to understand wait times or availability.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselors with industry expertise on staff
Free pre-purchase counseling and homeownership education—no cost for core services
Soft credit pull with guidance available for a small fee rather than blocking clients from understanding credit readiness
One-on-one personalized counseling rather than group classes only
Pre-foreclosure counseling available for homeowners facing potential loss
High-cost loan counseling to help clients avoid predatory lending
Clear intake process—online form submission with follow-up appointment scheduling
cons: Website lacks details on service geographic boundaries—unclear if they serve entire Louisiana or only Lafayette area
No information about wait times, availability, or how quickly appointments can be scheduled
Does not appear to provide direct down payment assistance or grants—only counseling about where to find them
Limited transparency on counseling session length, number of sessions included, or expected outcomes
Website lacks outcomes data or success metrics showing homeownership rates of clients served
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Lafayette/Acadiana area unsure if they're financially ready
Individuals with poor or unknown credit scores seeking guidance before applying for mortgages
Homeowners facing foreclosure seeking counseling and alternatives
Borrowers concerned about high-cost or predatory loan offers
diagnosis: Step Up Acadiana is ideal for prospective homebuyers in the Lafayette, Louisiana area who need objective, expert guidance to prepare financially and mentally for home ownership before approaching lenders. The main caveat is that this is pure counseling and education—they do not provide loans, down payment assistance, or grants directly, only guidance on accessing external programs, so applicants must be prepared to do additional legwork to fund their purchase.
services: Pre-foreclosure counseling for homeowners at risk
High-cost loan counseling and predatory lending guidance
Soft credit pulls with credit score insight (small fee)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 601 Loire Ave Ste H, Lafayette, LA 70507
